title: Laevo Mortar and Pestle Large Set - Black Granite Stone Spice Grinder, 2.1 Cup Capacity, 5.5 Inches - Reversible Molcajete Mexicano - Guacamole, Spices - Large Mortar and Pestles Set
description:
About this item
Durability and Stability: Crafted from solid granite; this mortar and pestle offers a durable, heavyweight design; 17 oz capacity; ensures stability for grinding spices, herbs, and more with ease
Includes Spoon and Lid: Comes with a spoon and silicone lid that doubles as a placemat; protect your countertop; keep ingredients fresh while preparing pestos, salsas, and more with your mortar pestle
Reversible Design: Features a unique reversible design; separate flavors and spices in one set; ideal for preparing various dishes like guacamole, pesto, or grinding spices in your molcajete mexicano
Unpolished and Non-Porous: Unpolished surface for optimal grinding; non-porous granite prevents absorption; ideal for making healthy homemade meals with this versatile pestle and mortar set
Thoughtful Gift Idea: A gift in stylish packaging; suitable for chefs and home cooks alike; ideal for housewarming, birthdays, and special occasions; durable and practical mortar and pestle sets
reviews_5s:
The Laevo White Marble Mortar and Pestle Set (Large) is an exceptional kitchen tool that combines functionality with elegance. The marble construction is not only aesthetically pleasing but also incredibly durable. The solid weight of the mortar provides stability, making grinding spices, herbs, and even tougher ingredients like nuts and garlic a breeze. The pestle is comfortable to hold and has just the right heft to make crushing and grinding effortless.

One of the standout features of this set is its versatility; it’s perfect for creating everything from spice blends to homemade pastes and sauces. The marble material is also easy to clean, and it doesn’t absorb odors or flavors, ensuring that your ingredients remain pure. The large size is perfect for preparing generous amounts, whether you're cooking for a family or a dinner party. This mortar and pestle set is a must-have for anyone serious about cooking.Arrived in perfect condition, in 3 days to Hawaii, so that’s great. It is smaller than expected, but it's so heavy, that’s actually a good thing...if it were any bigger, I would have a hard time handling it. At 69, my hands aren't as strong as they used too be. It's so pretty, I love it. I came with extras, a cute little wooden spoon that works surprisingly well to scoop out dried herbs and other things and a little silicone mat to keep the molcajeté from moving around or scratching your counter. However, it's very smooth so I don't believe it would scratch anything. I've only used it on dried Mexican Oregano, so far, but it works very well in helping one achieve their desired coarseness for their dish. Sometimes you need a spice or herb coarse, medium or finely ground and this will accomplish all that. It says the larger end holds 2.1 cups, but mine only held about 1.75 cups of water, to the absolute very top. I don't see that being a problem. It's well made, pretty and I am very happy with it. I highly recommend it.I love this mortar and pestle. It was packaged well and it is impressively large and in charge.

It has two mortar "zones". One is large and you simply flip it over to use the smaller bowl. The pestle is similarly two-sided and you can use whichever end suits the particular purpose at hand.

It also comes with a circular rubber/silicone pad that prevents the mortar from slipping.

It's made of solid granite and I'm confident it will outlast me. It also looks great sitting on the counter.

It weighs 2750 grams (6 lbs, 1 oz) so it's pretty solid. The pestle weighs 625 grams (1 lb, 6 ozs) so be careful not to drop it on your toes!Wow, this is one serious piece of rock. In hindsight, I should have ordered the smaller size. I got the large (5.6'') dark marble version. It looks fantastic, and I like the modern style, so different from the typical "same as they've always been since the Bronze Age" mortar and pestle look.

This thing is kind of the ultimate in mortar and pestles. I almost feel like I could grind up concrete in this thing. I like that it has a shallow side, which with the wider, flatter end of the pestle will be good for things like coriander and cumin seed and such.

If your goal is more toward guacamole and masa usage instead of spice-grinder, you probably do want the large version, but in the granite, which is rougher, more toward a traditional molcajete. Though if you're all about guac and masa, you should probably just get an actual molcajete.

Mine arrived without any damage. Unlike some prior reviewers, I got one that did not have any glue-patched cracks. I guess the manufacturer has stopped doing that in response to complaints, or I just got lucky. The box included a note to contact the mfr. for an immediate replacement if it arrives damaged. You just supply them a picture of the broken part. I would surmise you don't have to send it back to them. However, if you get one with a glued crack, I don't know if they'll respond the same way, and you might have to do an Amazon return if you don't trust the factory repair they did. Anyway, this IS natural stone; it will have some irregularities and imperfections. However, I find the grinding surfaces on each side of each tool to be even, effective, and exactly what I expected.

This is definitely a gift-quality piece, appropriate for someone who cooks (for real, from scratch) a lot and for a family, and who is not arthritic or otherwise weakened, since this is a large, heavy piece. For a little ol' granny, or someone living alone in an apartment, a mortar and pestle half this size would be quite sufficient. If you need one for grinding medications, not for (or not just for) cooking, then get a steel one, since medicines should not get into food, but a stone m&p will retain some since it's porous at the microscopic level.What a joy to cook with a piece of art. This thing is heavy and beautiful (I got the dark grey). The two sides of the mortor and the pestle are clever and provide options. The no-slip silicone base is also clever and protects surfaces as well. This would make a fantastic wedding present, or gift for someone with a new home or who loves to cook. It’ll last forever.Nice and sturdy, heavy, 2-sided mortar and 2-sided pestle. Comes with a little wooden spoon and a rubber lid for covering something you are not ready to remove from it right away.I am not sure what the purpose of the cover was, I repurposed it. since I store the pestle inside the mortar, it was just getting in the way. I do like the fact that it has straight walls (rather than bowl-shaped) which makes it easier to store.

Other than that, is a good, large set and does what is expected. I may want to order a smaller alternative for smaller jobs, but this one works as advertised for its size.My son requested this as a birthday gift. It's lovely and practical. He will receive it this week. I'm sure he'll love it!Very sturdy and flexible with real marble.Exactly what I wanted. Great size, great weight, and looks fantastic on the counter
reviews_1s:
Original Review:
I really liked the look, quality and many functions of this item. I wanted so bad to get a good one. I also read the numerous reviews talking about big cracks and broken parts. Well it happened to me. As you can see from the pics I put up there is an obvious crack starting on top going down the side and ending back about 3 inches away on top once again. You can see where someone tried to repair it on the side not a very good one (I could still feel the crack) but one on the start and ending cracks on the top were not repaired at all. If it were just one small crack repaired I would have been OK with it but really 3 to 4 inch crack. If it was used I might of bought it for less, but I'm not taking a chance that the side will fall off someday.
I'm returning it and giving 1 star not because it not a good item, but because they tried to slide one by me and have me go to UPS to return it. I'm not reordering it. Instead I'm ordering a very similar one with the white and black marble look for $.50 more and hoping it's not cracked. If so I'll order the granite one. Cross your fingers for me.
Updated Review
Well it's 3 days later and my new Mortar and Pestle that I reordered is here. It's not the same color scheme. It's more white and black marble look. Mostly white. This one is in perfect condition and all the items and bonus items arrived with it as well. Made by the same company. I'm a much happier camper with the new one. Hoping they sell the other one as a damaged deal. Some people just won't go the extra mile by returning it by driving to UPS and reordering a new one. Good luck if you still want to buy this dark colored one. Seems like the darker material is more fragile.Not sure how to contact seller but, for no reason, the mortar is chipping all the way around the inside rim. It’s been used ever so gently.See the picture that compares this M&P (on the right) with another brand.

1. SHORT PESTLE. This Pestle is too short and I ended up busting my knuckles a few times.

2. SMALL RADIUS. In addition, the Pestle's grinding side that's meant for the deeper side of the Mortar, has a surface with a very small radius. This results in more difficulty in grinding when using the circular motion for crushing (as opposed to up-down).

3. TWO SIDES NOT USEFUL. Practically speaking, one would rarely use the bottom side of the mortar since it is so shallow that food will bounce out easily. Besides, the deeper side is just as easy to use (in fact easier as you don't have to flip the heavy unit) even for smaller quantities of food.

I finally ended up buying this one: https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B06W2KBC4Z/The first time I ordered it came damaged with a large CRACK down the middle. I returned the item and received a replacement the next day which WAS ALSO DAMAGED. 2 times and both damaged… not much else to say2 months and 2 days after purchase the pestle chipped, cracked, then crumbled in the same use. Only use was guacamole, if it cannot handle mashing avocados long term, then it most definitely cannot work with harder materials.I bought his thinking good things and then... it broke while making pesto... yep crushing little old basil leaves... I hope they respond to this review. They didn't when I tried other avenues.

......Hmmm can't get a response from the seller.... it broke and is past the time of return....3 months of ownership nowThis product is made of marble, it came in pieces, I never dared to take it out of the box as it can have very sharp edges.
A slip of paper on top of box offered to ship a new one for free if broken.
No guarantee, even if it's one piece, if you break it while using you can cut yourself.Never got to use it. Product arrived damaged.My set had a large crack running through the Mortar. Not just a visible line, an actual deep crack which you could feel on both the inside and outside. The instructions that come with the set state that if you have any defects, contact the manufacturer (Laevo) and they will send out a replacement promptly. I contacted them, twice, they never even responded, let alone replaced anything. I had to send it back to Amazon. Poor quality control, poor customer service.I bought this just a few months ago and only used it 6 or 8 times.

As I was crushing a head of garlic it breaks into all the garlic... I was not hitting it hard to anything like that. I don’t understand how this happened. It’s a beautiful product and very heavy but it did not last at all.
